content.full_text:
March 17, 1982

John Kroeger  
Assembly Committee on Business & Professions  
California State Assembly  
State Capitol, Rm. 5175  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Telephone Advice

Dear Mr. Kroeger:

Thank you for your recent request for telephone advice. This letter will confirm my advice to you. You had asked whether any conflict of interest problems would arise if a legislative staff person (committee consultant) were to accept outside employment (moonlight) as a lobbyist for pay for another organization.

I advised you that it is the Commission's position that this would constitute using one's official position to influence a governmental decision under the Political Reform Act and our regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700. Receiving pay for doing this would give rise to the requisite financial interest under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(B).

In addition, I suggest to you that the individual read the Legislature's Code of Ethics, found at Government Code Sections 8920 through 8926.

If I can be of further assistance or answer any other questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Robert E. Leidigh  
Counsel  
Legal Division

REL:km  
Enclosures: §18700 and §18702

sections.question: Whether any conflict of interest problems would arise if a legislative staff person (committee consultant) were to accept outside employment (moonlight) as a lobbyist for pay for another organization.

Adm. Code Section 18700. Receiving pay for doing this would give rise to the requisite financial interest under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(B). sections.question_synthetic: Whether a legislative staff person can ethically accept outside employment as a paid lobbyist for another organization without creating conflicts of interest.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that a legislative staff person accepting paid outside employment as a lobbyist would violate the Political Reform Act by using their official position to influence governmental decisions and creating prohibited financial interests. The individual should also review the Legislature's Code of Ethics.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Telephone advice confirmation letter with clear Q&C structure embedded in narrative form. Document cites specific regulatory sections (2 Cal. Adm. Code §18700 and §18702). Enclosures referenced but not included in provided text.
